# Nick Cardoso

> Software engineer building tools, scripts, and experiments across modern web technologies, Rust, Linux desktop applications, and scalable backend services. Portfolio at nickcardoso.com.

## Projects

- [Mimick](https://link.nickcardoso.com/projects/mimick/): Premier unofficial Immich desktop client for Linux -- native GTK4 app built in Rust with background sync, watch folders, OCR search, and queue inspector.
- [HyTrack API](https://link.nickcardoso.com/projects/hytrackapi/): Hardened FastAPI microservice for courier status scraping with key management, rate limiting, and edge-ready deployment.
- [HyTrackV3](https://link.nickcardoso.com/projects/hytrackv3/): Email-driven shipment intelligence engine that monitors waybills and pushes status-change notifications.
- [ImmichSync](https://link.nickcardoso.com/projects/immichsync/): Python-based folder-to-Immich sync automation with route fallback, deduplication, and album-aware uploads.
- [InfinityX](https://link.nickcardoso.com/projects/infinityx/): ESPHome-powered hardware mod that turns a Bluetooth speaker into a Home Assistant controllable device.
- [Lets Watch](https://link.nickcardoso.com/projects/letswatch/): Local-first watch-party app for self-hosted environments that syncs video playback over Socket.IO.
- [MultiWave](https://link.nickcardoso.com/projects/multiwave/): Wireless dual-node cable tester for RJ45, RJ11, and BNC diagnostics with multiple validation modes.
- [Picoaudio](https://link.nickcardoso.com/projects/picoaudio/): Bluetooth A2DP audio receiver for the Raspberry Pi Pico 2 W that outputs 16-bit stereo PCM over I2S.
- [Room VSA](https://link.nickcardoso.com/projects/roomvsa/): High-performance, client-side web application designed for 3D room visualization and interior design.
- [Unstats](https://link.nickcardoso.com/projects/unstats/): Home Assistant integration for Unsplash account metrics with a privacy-preserving relay architecture.
- [Voltas IR Mapping](https://link.nickcardoso.com/projects/voltas-ir/): Complete IR code database, decoded protocol documentation, and ready-to-use Home Assistant integration for the Voltas 183V DZU2 AC.
- [WaveDisp](https://link.nickcardoso.com/projects/wavedisp/): Interactive control dashboard and rendering engine for Raspberry Pi LED matrix displays.

## Source Code

- [GitHub Profile](https://github.com/nicx17): All open-source repositories and contributions.
